content:
I understand your frustration behind the ranking system, especially after the real-time contest when climbing leagues becomes much harder.

However, the puzzle statement currently reads as if the described behaviour reflects the real arena ranking system. Since the real system uses TrueSkill (note 1) and handles draws in a variable way (note 2), it could be misleading for players who only see the statement and not your comment here that explains the intent.

Please revise the statement so it clearly frames the three rules as simplified or hypothetical models inspired by the arena, rather than implying they describe the actual ranking mechanism or an actual committee that decides the mechanism.

You may also want to check the official explanations of the ranking system and draw handling for reference:
• https://forum.codingame.com/t/multiplayer-contests-ranking-system/381/6
• https://forum.codingame.com/t/ultimate-tic-tac-toe-puzzle-discussion/22616/13

Also, I know that the real CG scoring system is slightly more complex. This problem reduces its complexity but still clearly demonstrates why handling draws properly might be the key to correct and STABLE ratings. Most of you know how much harder is to beat Silver/Gold league after ending of the real-time contest. It's mostly because of the draws. So the problem is like, you know, some meta-irony on this order of things. Don't know really, is it needed to inverse the loops order to show that the LATEST player will gain worst score on draws or it is obvious by itself.
Question: is it hard or medium? The solution is not very tricky. But needs some understanding of underlying problem as I think.
